content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Jen Slater 
Treasurer 
Campaign Compliance Group 
9070 Irvine Center Drive, Suite 150 
Irvine, CA 92618 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-15-236 
Dear Ms. Slater: 
January 14, 2016 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding campaign provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Please note that we can only provide advice as to the provisions 
of the Act and its regulations; the Commission does not advise on a requestor's duties under a local 
ordinance. 
QUESTIONS 
l. 
May "Shawn Nelson for Supervisor 2014," an officeholder controlled committee, accept 
contributions that, due to an administrative error, are addressed to "Shawn Nelson for Supervisor 
2018"? 
2. 
Can an officeholder committee and bank account be redesignated for a future election 
simply by amending the year in the committee's name, without filing an amended Form 410 
Statement of Organization and a new Form 501 Candidate Intention Statement? 
CONCLUSIONS 
I. 
Yes. Under these facts, "Shawn Nelson for Supervisor 2014" may accept contributions 
addressed to "Shawn Nelson for Supervisor 2018," because the contributions are intended for the 
2014 committee, which but for an administrative error, would not have changed its name. 
2. 
No. To redesignate a campaign committee and bank account, an officeholder must file a 
new Form 501 indicating the officeholder's intention to run for a future election, and then file an 
amended Form 410 redesignating the committee for a future election. 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS 
File No. A-15-
Page No. 2 
In June of 2014, Shawn Nelson was re-elected to a final four-year tenn as Orange County 
Supervisor for the Fourth District. You are president of Campaign Compliance Group and serve as 
treasurer for Supervisor Nelson's campaign committee, "Shawn Nelson for Supervisor 2014" ID## 
1316599. On January 1, 2015, you submitted a Form 410, requesting to change the committee's 
name to "Shawn Nelson for Supervisor 2014-2018." You did not file a new Fonn 501 indicating 
Supervisor Nelson's intention to run for a future election, 
The Secretary of State rejected your name change request because the proposed name 
contained two different election years. In response, you changed the committee's name to "Shawn 
Nelson for Supervisor 2018.'' You did so to indicate that the committee was for the officeholder 
2014-2018 period, as opposed to for campaign debt. You then changed the committee's name back 
to "Shawn Nelson for Supervisor 2014," since Supervisor Nelson's term as County Supervisor ends 
on December 31, 2018, at which point he will be ineligible to run for reelection. 
Before the final name change, you received contributions addressed to "Shawn Nelson for 
Supervisor 2018.'' The issue is now whether the committee under its current name, '"Shawn Nelson 
for Supervisor 2014," may accept contributions addressed to the erroneously named "Shawn Nelson 
for Supervisor 2018," or whether changing the committee's name to include a future election year 
constituted a redesignation of the account for a future election. 
ANALYSIS 
1. Officeholder Accounts 
Under the Act, a candidate controlled committee is a committee set up by a candidate or 
officeholder for his or her election. "Shawn Nelson for Supervisor 2014" was set up for Supervisor 
Nelson's election, so it is a candidate controlled committee for purposes of the Act. 
Under Section 85201 of the Act, a candidate or officeholder may only have one controlled 
committee and one corresponding bank account per election. (Hicks Advice Letter, No. 1-99-120.). 
However, an officeholder may use the campaign committee from the current term of office for 
officeholder related expenses. An officeholder expense is any expense related to a legislative or 
governmental purpose, such as using campaign funds to supplement governmental staff salaries. 
(Tavaglione Advice Letter, No A-05-166). 
In January of 2015, you sought to change the name of"Shawn Nelson for Supervisor 2014," 
to "Shawn Nelson for Supervisor 2014-2018" in order to clarify that the committee's purpose was 
to pay for expenses associated with holding office during his term from 2014-2018, as opposed to 
outstanding campaign debt from Supervisor Nelson's last election. Assuming it is pennitted by 
local law, we have advised that an officeholder may use his or her committee for election to the 
current tenn of office for expenditures associated with holding office. 
Accordingly, "Shawn Nelson for Supervisor 2014" meets the naming requirements for 
officeholder controlled committees and may be used for officeholder expenditures for the 


File No. A-15-
Page No. 3 
supervisor's current tenn of office. But "S.hawn Nelson for Supervisor 2018" is technically 
inaccurate, since Supervisor Nelson was elected to his current tenn of office in 2014, not 2018. Yet 
Supervisor Nelson cannot seek reelection to the same office, since his second tenn ends December 
31, 2018, and Orange County limits County Supervisors to two four-year tenns. 
Thus, Supervisor Nelson clearly intends to solicit contributions to cover officeholder 
expenses for the period of 2014-2018, not to finance a campaign for a future election. The 
committee may therefore accept contributions addressed to "Shawn Nelson for Supervisor 2018," 
because but for an administrative error, these contributions are clearly intended for "Shawn Nelson 
for Supervisor 2014.'' In addition, you note that you plan to notify donors of the correct committee 
name. 
2. Redesignation for a future election 
A local officeholder seeking reelection to the same office can use the same bank account 
that he or she established for his or her prior election by "redesignating" the account. The 
officeholder can redesignate the account at any time before receiving contributions in connection 
with reelection. To do so, the officeholder must file a new Fonn 501 (Candidate Intention 
Statement) for a specific future election, along with an amended Fonn 410 (Statement of 
Organization) redesignating the officeholder account for the new election. 
Though you changed the name of Supervisor Nelson's controlled committee from "Shawn 
Nelson for Supervisor 2014," to "Shawn Nelson for Supervisor 2018," you did not file a new Fonn 
501. Therefore, you did not redesignate the controlled committee or bank account for a future 
election. Even if you had attempted to redesignate the committee and bank account for a future 
election, Supervisor Nelson cannot run for reelection in 2018, so such a redesignation would have 
been meaningless. 
An officeholder committee or bank account can only be redesignated for a future election by 
filing an amended Form 410 and a new Form 501. Because you did not file a new Form 501 and 
there was an administrative mistake concerning the committee name, we find that you may accept 
contributions mistakenly addressed to "Shawn Nelson for Supervisor 2018" to use for officeholder 
expenses associated with Supervisor Nelson's remaining term of office. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
TAL:jgl 
Sincerely, 
. Wagner 
Counsel 
By: 
Toren A. Lewis 
Counsel, Legal Division
